confidence_breakdown.calculation_notes: CVE-2021-36258 (Poly Network) + real breaches (Poly Network, Ronin Bridge) + smart contract audit reports + production testing + bug bounty programs

owasp_top10: OWASP Smart Contract Top 10 - SC06: Insufficient Logging and Monitoring
OWASP API Security Top 10 - API10:2023 Unsafe Consumption of APIs
mitre_attack: T1562.001 - Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
cwe: CWE-117 - Improper Output Neutralization for Logs
CWE-345 - Insufficient Verification of Data Authenticity

threat.title: Blockchain Transaction Non-Repudiation Failure
threat.description: When smart contracts fail to emit comprehensive audit events or oracle data lacks cryptographic proof, attackers can dispute legitimate transactions or manipulate off-chain records without detection. This vulnerability enables transaction repudiation attacks where malicious actors deny performing actions, exploit incomplete event logging to hide fraudulent transfers, or manipulate oracle feeds to trigger unauthorized state changes while leaving no immutable audit trail. The absence of cryptographically signed transaction receipts and incomplete event emission makes forensic investigation impossible, allowing attackers to steal funds and escape accountability.

attack.scenario:
1. Attacker identifies smart contract with incomplete event logging for high-value transfers
2. Executes malicious transaction (e.g., unauthorized withdrawal) that modifies state but emits no events
3. Manipulates off-chain oracle data feed using front-running or flash loan attack
4. Oracle price manipulation triggers contract function without proper event emission
5. Victim discovers unauthorized state change but lacks on-chain proof due to missing events
6. Attacker denies responsibility, claiming transaction never occurred or was victim's error
7. Without immutable event logs or cryptographically signed receipts, forensic investigation fails
8. Attacker successfully repudiates transaction and escapes accountability while retaining stolen assets
prerequisites: Smart contract lacks comprehensive event emission for critical state changes
No cryptographic signing of transaction receipts by trusted parties
Oracle data feeds lack tamper-proof provenance and digital signatures
Off-chain audit systems not synchronized with on-chain state
Missing transaction replay protection or nonce verification
Insufficient monitoring of contract events in real-time SIEM

cve_references.description: Poly Network cross-chain bridge exploit where attacker manipulated keeper role without proper event logging, enabling $611M theft with disputed transaction origins
Qubit Finance bridge hack exploiting missing deposit event verification, resulting in $80M loss through phantom collateral claims

real_world_breaches.impact: $611 million stolen across Ethereum, BSC, and Polygon; attacker exploited missing event logs for keeper role changes, making transaction repudiation difficult
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Smart contract lacked proper event emission for EthCrossChainManager keeper modifications; off-chain monitoring could not detect unauthorized state changes in real-time
real_world_breaches.technical_details: Attacker called EthCrossChainData.putCurEpochConPubKeyBytes() to replace keeper public key without emitting KeeperChanged event, then signed malicious cross-chain messages. Absence of immutable audit trail enabled initial denial of transaction origins.

real_world_breaches.impact: $625 million stolen via compromised validators; incomplete transaction logs made forensic timeline reconstruction challenging during initial incident response
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Validator nodes lacked comprehensive audit logging for withdrawal approvals; missing event emission for multi-sig threshold changes allowed 5/9 validator takeover to go undetected
real_world_breaches.technical_details: Attacker compromised 5 validator private keys through social engineering; bridge contract emitted basic Transfer events but missed ValidatorApproval and ThresholdChanged events, delaying detection by 6 days. Transaction receipts lacked cryptographic signatures from independent witnesses.

real_world_breaches.impact: $325 million exploit through forged guardian signatures; missing oracle attestation logs enabled attacker to mint unbacked wrapped ETH
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Guardian network failed to emit cryptographically signed attestation events; signature verification bypass left no audit trail for forensic analysis
real_world_breaches.technical_details: Attacker exploited signature verification bug in verify_signatures function; contract emitted TokensMinted event but missing GuardianAttestation event prevented real-time detection. Off-chain guardian network logs were not synchronized with on-chain state.

real_world_breaches.impact: $190 million drained through 960+ transactions; incomplete event indexing caused SIEM alert fatigue and delayed incident response
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Trusted root misconfiguration combined with missing WithdrawalValidation events; attackers replayed transactions without proper audit trail

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Missing event emission in DeFi protocol withdrawal function allowed $2M unauthorized transfer without audit trail; researcher demonstrated transaction repudiation attack
Chainlink oracle data feed manipulation due to missing signature verification events; attacker could dispute price updates

security_research.title: Inadequate Event Logging in Ethereum Smart Contracts
security_research.key_finding: 68% of audited smart contracts lack comprehensive event emission for state-changing functions, creating blind spots for forensic analysis

security_research.title: Oracle Manipulation and Non-Repudiation in DeFi
security_research.key_finding: Off-chain oracle data feeds without cryptographic attestation enable transaction repudiation attacks in 45% of DeFi protocols

security_research.title: OWASP Smart Contract Top 10: Insufficient Logging
security_research.key_finding: SC06 Insufficient Logging ranked #6 vulnerability; missing events prevent incident response and enable repudiation attacks

mitigations.title: Comprehensive Event Logging in Solidity Smart Contracts
mitigations.description: Implement indexed events for all state-changing functions with complete parameter logging, timestamps, and actor addresses. Use OpenZeppelin's AccessControl events and custom audit events for critical operations to create immutable on-chain audit trail.
nist_csf: PR.PT-1 - Audit/log records are determined and documented
DE.AE-3 - Event data are collected and correlated
validation_steps: Deploy contract to testnet and verify all events emit correctly
Use Hardhat expect().to.emit() to test event emission in unit tests
Query events using ethers.js or web3.js and verify indexed parameters
Set up The Graph subgraph to index all contract events
Perform state-changing transactions and confirm events in block explorer
Test transaction replay protection with duplicate withdrawal attempts

mitigations.title: Hyperledger Fabric Chaincode Audit Trail with CouchDB Rich Queries
mitigations.description: Implement comprehensive transaction logging in Hyperledger Fabric chaincode using composite keys, private data collections, and CouchDB rich queries for immutable audit trail. Store transaction metadata in ledger state with endorsement policies.
nist_csf: DE.AE-3 - Event data are collected and correlated
RS.AN-1 - Notifications from detection systems are investigated
validation_steps: Deploy chaincode to Fabric test network
Invoke createAsset and verify audit log created with composite key
Query audit logs using CouchDB rich query and verify results
Test getAssetHistory to retrieve immutable transaction history
Verify event emission using Fabric SDK event listener
Test transfer authorization with client certificates
Validate endorsement policy satisfaction for sensitive operations

mitigations.title: Web3 Transaction Receipt Verification with Cryptographic Proof
mitigations.description: Implement client-side transaction receipt verification using web3.js/ethers.js to obtain cryptographically signed receipts from Ethereum nodes. Store receipts in tamper-proof storage (IPFS, Arweave) with hash anchoring on-chain for non-repudiation.
nist_csf: PR.DS-6 - Integrity checking mechanisms are used
validation_steps: Test transaction execution and receipt retrieval
Verify audit record structure contains all required fields
Upload audit record to IPFS and retrieve using CID
Validate cryptographic proof verification logic
Test receipt hash calculation and tamper detection
Verify Merkle proof generation (if fully implemented)
Test audit record retrieval and hash verification

mitigations.title: Chainlink Oracle Data Attestation with Verifiable Random Function (VRF)
mitigations.description: Implement oracle data feeds with cryptographic signatures using Chainlink's decentralized oracle network. Store oracle responses with VRF proofs on-chain to prevent manipulation and enable non-repudiation of price data.
nist_csf: PR.DS-6 - Integrity checking mechanisms are used
DE.AE-5 - Incident alert thresholds are established
validation_steps: Deploy contract to testnet with Chainlink price feed
Request oracle data and verify attestation storage
Test VRF request and fulfillment with audit trail
Verify attestation integrity using verifyAttestation function
Test oracle manipulation detection with price jump scenarios
Retrieve audit trail and confirm all fields populated
Test replay protection with duplicate data hashes

indicators: Smart contract state changes without corresponding event emissions
Oracle price updates with abnormal deviations (>50% jumps)
Transactions with missing or incomplete event logs in block explorers
Discrepancies between on-chain state and off-chain monitoring systems
Validator signature anomalies in cross-chain bridge transactions
Unusually high transaction volumes without event log spikes
Failed transaction receipt verifications during audit
Missing transaction replay protection leading to duplicate processing

log_queries.query:
// Detect blockchain transactions with missing event emissions
BlockchainLogs
| where EventType == "Transaction" and Status == "Success"
| where EventEmissionCount == 0 or EventEmissionCount < ExpectedMinimumEvents
| extend TxHash = tostring(TransactionHash)
| extend ContractAddress = tostring(ToAddress)
| join kind=leftouter (
    BlockchainEvents
    | where EventType in ("Transfer", "Withdrawal", "StateChange")
    | summarize EventCount = count() by TransactionHash
) on $left.TxHash == $right.TransactionHash
| where isempty(EventCount) or EventCount == 0
| project TimeGenerated, TxHash, ContractAddress, FromAddress, Value, GasUsed, BlockNumber
| order by TimeGenerated desc

index=blockchain sourcetype=ethereum_transactions status=success
| join type=left txhash [
    search index=blockchain sourcetype=ethereum_events
    | stats count as event_count by txhash
]
| where isnull(event_count) OR event_count=0
| eval severity=case(
    value>1000000, "critical",
    value>100000, "high",
    1=1, "medium"
)
| table _time, txhash, from_address, to_address, value, gas_used, event_count, severity
| where severity in ("critical", "high")

event.category: "blockchain" AND event.outcome: "success" AND blockchain.transaction.type: "contract_interaction" AND NOT _exists_: blockchain.events
| stats count by blockchain.transaction.hash, blockchain.contract.address, blockchain.transaction.value
| where blockchain.transaction.value > 100000

fields @timestamp, transactionHash, contractAddress, fromAddress, value, gasUsed
| filter eventType = "Transaction" and status = "success"
| filter eventEmissionCount = 0 or isempty(eventEmissionCount)
| filter value > 1000000
| sort @timestamp desc
| limit 100

// Detect suspicious oracle price updates
BlockchainEvents
| where EventType == "OracleDataReceived" or EventType == "PriceUpdate"
| extend Price = toreal(EventData.answer)
| extend PriceFeedAddress = tostring(EventData.priceFeed)
| sort by TimeGenerated asc
| extend PreviousPrice = prev(Price, 1)
| extend PriceChangePercent = abs((Price - PreviousPrice) / PreviousPrice * 100)
| where PriceChangePercent > 50
| extend TimeSincePrevious = TimeGenerated - prev(TimeGenerated, 1)
| where TimeSincePrevious < 60s
| project TimeGenerated, PriceFeedAddress, Price, PreviousPrice, PriceChangePercent, TimeSincePrevious, TransactionHash
| order by TimeGenerated desc

index=blockchain sourcetype=bridge_transactions
| eval time_diff=_time-coalesce(last_validator_time, _time)
| where time_diff > 300 OR missing_validator_signatures > 0
| eval risk_score=case(
    missing_validator_signatures>=3, 100,
    missing_validator_signatures>=2, 75,
    time_diff>600, 60,
    1=1, 40
)
| where risk_score >= 60
| table _time, bridge_name, transaction_hash, source_chain, dest_chain, amount, missing_validator_signatures, risk_score
| sort -risk_score

tools.name: Etherscan / Block Explorer
tools.usage: Verify transaction receipt, event logs, and internal transactions for comprehensive audit trail

tools.name: Tenderly
tools.usage: Simulate transactions before execution, inspect event emissions, debug failed transactions

tools.name: OpenZeppelin Defender
tools.usage: Real-time monitoring of contract events, automated alerting on suspicious activity, transaction simulation

tools.name: Forta Network
tools.usage: Deploy detection bots for missing event emissions, oracle manipulation, bridge anomalies

tools.name: Chainalysis
tools.usage: Investigate suspicious transactions, trace fund flows, identify malicious actors
